hero.title: I'm Tan Ngoc — a freelance fullstack developer based in Vietnam.
hero.subtitle: I help founders and small teams ship web products that feel cared-for. Six years of writing software, and I still get excited every Monday.

story.title: How I got here
paragraphs: I started building websites in high school because I wanted to share what I was learning. One Wordpress theme later, I was hooked on the loop of ship → see people use it → fix.

I spent four years at product companies in Ho Chi Minh City — first on a fintech dashboard, then on a logistics platform. Both taught me to care about the boring half of the job: data models, deploys, and the long tail of edge cases.
Now I freelance full-time. Most of my work is helping founders go from idea to launch, or rescuing products that have outgrown their first codebase.

items.title: Freelance fullstack developer
items.description: Quizzle, Shopline, Tortuga and a few other clients I can't name yet.

items.title: Senior engineer · Logix
items.description: Led the rebuild of a last-mile logistics dispatcher used by 400+ drivers daily.

items.title: Fullstack engineer · Finhub
items.description: Owned the merchant onboarding flow and refactored the payments queue end-to-end.

items.title: Ship weekly
items.description: If you can't see it on a staging URL, it isn't progress.

items.title: Boring tech, sharp execution
items.description: I pick proven tools and spend the saved energy on the details users feel.

items.title: Honest scope
items.description: I'd rather say 'not this quarter' than miss a deadline you planned around.

items.title: Leave things tidy
items.description: Tests where they matter, docs for the next person, no leftover TODOs.
